`proposed+supersedes` composability is undocumented: when both flags are passed together, it is unspecified whether the superseded ADR's `Superseded by:` line should be updated immediately (when the new ADR is still Status: Proposed) or deferred until the user accepts the ADR — surfaced as VQ-S4-009 PARTIAL, pipeline-autonomous-retros S4

findings.text: No `skills/languages/markdown.md` exists; the verification-examiner's AC-derived SPEC protocol requires "(b) tests at file:line" and "(c) confirmation tests are not pure mock-spy proxies", written for executable code — epics whose stories edit only Markdown files have no matching language skill, causing examiners to improvise — surfaced during epic-next-modes

findings.text: Mode-3 verification-question schema is split between two conflicting shapes: the system-prompt canonical shape uses `"question_id"/"category"/"ac_id"/"phase"` (no `check_method`), while the task-specific prompt uses `"id"/"type"/"ac_ref"/"check_method"/"status"`; every on-disk `verification.json` uses `"type"` not `"category"`, and `check_method` is absent from the system-prompt schema; recommendation: adopt the richer task-specific shape, rename `id→question_id` and `ac_ref→ac_id` to align vocabulary, ship `verification.schema.json` so drift is caught at write time; update `base:verification-examiner` in the same change — surfaced across epic-next-modes and epic-pipeline-autonomous-retros Mode-3 planning

findings.text: Mode-1 story-planner should require a reconciliation pass against `exploration.json` before finalizing ACs: AC-MANIFEST-1 in epic-base-next pointed at non-existent commands listings in plugin.json/marketplace.json that the explorer had already flagged as absent, but the AC was not updated; add a Mode-1 step that cross-checks every AC's referenced artifact (file paths, manifest keys, config entries) against explorer findings — surfaced during epic-base-next pre-implementation

findings.text: SKIP condition "one-off interactive debugging where sequential execution adds no value" is too easy to misapply: repeated single-file targeted runs (e.g. `npx playwright test e2e/46-...`) get reclassified as debugging when they are in fact repeated suite invocations; practical consequence was 11 tests running in parallel against a shared relay each time, full output flooding main context, and no fail-fast discipline; tighten the SKIP condition to require *truly* one-off (≤1 run, non-suite) or add a heuristic: if the same test file is invoked more than once in a session, escalate to the skill — surfaced in a nostr-skills session

deferred.detail: ## Solution and acceptance-criteria.md are un-authored stubs

findings.text: `/base:bug` leaves working tree dirty after ACCEPTED: when a run terminates ACCEPTED with no escalation, no rejection, and no open remediation rounds, the natural terminal state is "committed to branch" not "staged for review"; Step 4 should auto-commit exactly the files in the contract's `allowed_changes.files` plus bug-reports artifacts and BACKLOG.md, using the bug slug + root_cause for the commit message; skip auto-commit on any non-ACCEPTED terminal state (ESCALATED, REJECTED, ABORT:UNDERSPECIFIED); surfaced by user after shophop/waitForTimeout-has-no-durable-fallback run

deferred.detail: ## Solution and acceptance-criteria.md are entirely un-auth…

findings.text: `/base:feature` leaves working tree dirty on successful completion: all-stories-ACCEPTED runs end without committing, requiring a manual commit step; the final phase should auto-commit the epic's changed files using the epic slug as the commit message, skipping on ESCALATED or ABORT terminal states

deferred.detail: ## Solution is an empty stub; no intent authored

findings.text: AC verification commands must distinguish pre-fix from post-fix state: during epic-story-planner-schema-conformance planning, `grep 'category'` would have passed as a false-green for AC-STRUCT-4 before the fix because the JSON example block already contained the field name; the tightened command (`grep -n 'not.*"type"'` with line-number guard) caught this, but the principle is not documented — Mode-3 VQ authors and AC authors should verify that every grep/shell check returns a different result on a clean checkout vs. after the fix; surfaced during epic-story-planner-schema-conformance Mode 1 planning

Schema-fixing epics produce a `stories.json` that cannot be validated against the very schema pattern being fixed during the run: the schema is in a broken state when the story-planner writes `stories.json`, so the schema cannot describe its own repair artifact; this structural catch-22 recurs for any epic whose sole deliverable is a schema pattern change — story-planner Mode 2 should note that schema-repair stories are exempt from runtime schema validation during their own run; surfaced during epic-story-planner-schema-conformance Mode 2 planning

Mode-3 TEST and SPEC verification questions are indistinguishable for documentation-only stories (no automated test suite): when all story verification is grep/shell commands on Markdown files, TEST questions and SPEC questions produce identical check commands with no meaningful categorical difference; story-planner Mode 3 should note that for Markdown-only stories, TEST questions may be collapsed into SPEC or omitted with an explicit rationale; surfaced during epic-story-planner-schema-conformance Mode 3 planning

`epic-state.json` has no schema file unlike `verification.json`, `result.json`, and `stories.json`: the `pre_existing_dirty_files` field added in the verifier-cannot-distinguish-pre-existing fix is purely convention-based with no validation barrier — a schema at `plugins/base/schemas/epic-state.schema.json` would catch field-name drift between feature.md and verification-examiner.md at write time; surfaced during bug fix verifier-cannot-distinguish-pre-existing

`feature.md:701` spawn paragraph describes the PARTIAL trigger and the hard-violation path but does not explicitly state the hard violation takes precedence when both co-occur in the same diff — the invariant is stated at `verification-examiner.md:55` but not mirrored in the spawn paragraph, creating documentation-drift risk; fix: add one sentence to `feature.md:701` ("When a diff contains both a non-baseline out-of-scope file and a baseline-overlap file, the hard violation always wins — PARTIAL is the answer only when no non-baseline out-of-scope file is in the diff") or reduce the spawn paragraph to "pass the array; see verification-examiner.md for usage semantics" to avoid duplication; surfaced by reviewer follow-up during verifier-cannot-distinguish-pre-existing bug fix
